title: calamus oblongus subsp. gracilis
intro: Calamus oblongus subsp. gracilis, also known as sweet calamus, is a subspecies of the Acorus calamus plant, a perennial herb native to Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of sweet calamus include relief from digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, as well as a reduction in anxiety and stress levels. Sweet calamus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including essential oil, tinctures, and dried roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams.
